Expert Tree Care in Charlotte

Certified arborists serving Mecklenburg County since 2011. Specializing in tree health assessments, safe removals, and storm damage response.

Call Now: 555-tre-rq7

Get a FREE Estimate Today!

Rooted in Charlotte's Community

Founded by a third-generation arborist, Summit Tree Service combines traditional techniques with modern ISA standards. Our team grew from one truck servicing South End to a 20-person crew covering five counties.

Our Tree Service Services

Professional tree service solutions tailored to your needs.

Safe Tree Removal

Precision dismantling of hazardous trees with minimal property impact.

Learn More →

Structural Pruning

Health-focused trimming that respects natural tree form.

Learn More →

Stump Removal

Complete below-grade elimination without damaging surrounding landscape.

Learn More →

Storm Damage Response

24/7 triage for hurricane, ice storm, and tornado damage.

Learn More →

Professional Tree Installation

Right tree, right place - tailored to Charlotte soils.

Learn More →

Frequently Asked Questions

Get answers to the most common questions about our tree service services.

How do you protect my lawn during removal?

We use 3/4-inch plywood mats to distribute equipment weight, especially important for Charlotte's erosion-prone clay soils. For spring removals when the ground is soft, we'll suggest alternate access points.

What happens to the wood?

Hardwoods go to local sawmills for lumber, softer species become playground mulch for CMS schools. We never send usable wood to landfills.

Reviews From Our Tree Service Customers

See what our satisfied customers have to say about their experience.

"When a 60-foot pine crushed our garage during the April hailstorm, Summit had it cleared before the insurance adjuster arrived. Their crew protected our garden beds while working and even saved two damaged dogwoods by cabling them."

Marcus T. Emergency storm damage removal

"The 100-year-old oak shading our Dilworth porch was dropping dangerous limbs. Summit's arborist explained how weight reduction pruning would help more than topping. Two years later, the tree looks majestic and hasn't lost a single branch in storms."

Elena R. Historic oak pruning

"Five companies refused to tackle the massive sweetgum stump in our backyard near Freedom Park. Summit's compact grinder fit through our narrow gate and they went 18 inches deep - no suckers have appeared all summer."

Devon W. Stump grinding
